Intero Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion For Evidentiary Sanctions Against Eagle Defendants For Spoliation Of Evidence
Off Calendar Aug-13-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances ((by Zoom):
Alexander Ramon, Esq. 408-947-2430 of Hoge Fenton for Moving Party/Defendant INTERO REAL ESTATE SERVICES, INC, WACHIRAPORN "JANE" WACHIRADEJKUL, DOUGLAS TOBIN
Shahrad Milanfar, Esq. 925-433-6003 for Defendant Eagle Properties.
Court Reporter: Joann Broadwell, CSR 10959, 510-332-0512, broadwelljo@aol.com, Reported.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

RULING - A R G U E D.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT INTERO REAL ESTATE SERVICES, INC, WACHIRAPORN "JANE" WACHIRADEJKUL, DOUGLAS TOBIN's Motion For Evidentiary Sanctions Against Eagle Defendants For Spoliation Of Evidence.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Intero Real Estate Services et al. (Intero) requested that Eagle Property Solutions et al. (Eagle) produce an email dated November 15, 2016 in native format with all metadata intact. Eagle responded that it was unable to do so because it was "no longer able to access the email account marion@eaglepsllc.com used to send [the email] in 2016," and "[a]s a result was unable to provide [the email] in the format requested." Intero asserts that Eagle has spoliated evidence and seeks evidentiary sanctions in the form of an order establishing that Eagle "never sent the email" and that the November 15, 2016 timestamp on the email "is a forgery." The email in question purports to have been sent on November 15, 2016 (seven months before the action was filed) from "marion@eaglepsllc.com" to "janewsfo@gmail.com," "mmatundan@icloud.com" and "Jenae.lozado@yahoo.com." It appears to have been subsequently forwarded on July 2, 2019 (more than two years after the action was filed) from "marion@eaglepsllc.com" to "marionmatundan@outlook.com." Eagle asserts that eleven months later, in June 2020, it no longer had access to the email account "marion@eaglepsllc.com," but offers no explanation how or why access to the account was "lost" notwithstanding Eagle's obligation to preserve evidence relevant to the pending litigation. Neither party discusses whether the original email is available in native format at janewsfo@gmail.com, mmatundan@icloud.com, Jenae.lozado@yahoo.com or marionmatundan@outlook.com. The record on this motion is insufficient to establish spoliation or to determine what remedy, if any, is appropriate. "[I]n most cases of purported spoliation the facts should be decided and any appropriate inference should be made by the trier of fact after a full hearing at trial." New Albertsons, Inc. v. Superior Court, 168 Cal.App.4th 1403, 1431 (2008). The motion is denied, without prejudice to a motion in limine to the trial court, which will be able to evaluate the issues presented on a fuller record and fashion a remedy, if warranted.

Mr. Milanfar is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem in word format at rmead@folgerlevin.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Roger Mead, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Joann Broadwell, CSR 10959, 510-332-0512, broadwelljo@aol.com, Reported. =(302/JPT)
